analyst_observations: with_skill achieves 100% pass rate vs 93.3% baseline — the only failure was the structured summary table assertion on eval-1 baseline
The skill's output format is significantly more structured: inline [CATEGORY][severity] labels, markdown summary table, and 'What's Good' section are consistent across all 3 runs
Baseline reviews are high-quality but free-form — good content coverage, weaker on output consistency and GitHub-ready formatting
The skill uses ~44% more tokens (15003 vs 10386 avg) due to richer formatting and more extensive inline comment blocks
The skill is ~72% slower (69734ms vs 40518ms avg) — the extra time is spent on structured analysis phases
Eval-2 and Eval-3 were perfect for both configurations — those assertions may be too easy (both catch N+1, path traversal, etc.). The differentiating assertion is the summary table in eval-1.
The 'What's Good' section (calling out correct use of parameterized queries, sensible response shapes) only appeared in the with-skill outputs — adds meaningful positivity absent in baseline
